overview: The Right to Information (RTI) Act 2005 empowers every Indian citizen to request information from any government authority. It promotes transparency and accountability in government functioning. Any citizen can ask for documents, records, or inspection of any government work. The fee is only Rs 10. BPL card holders are exempt from fees.

eligibility.who_can_file: Any Indian citizen can file an RTI application
eligibility.reason_required: No — applicant is not required to give any reason (Section 6(2))
eligibility.bpl_exemption: BPL card holders are exempted from fees — must attach BPL certificate
eligibility.section_reference: Section 3 and Section 6(2) of RTI Act 2005

what_you_can_ask: Copies of any government document or record
Details of where scheme funds were spent
Salary and posting details of government officers
Status of ration card, PF, pension, scholarship
Any information held by a public authority
what_you_cannot_ask: Cabinet papers and Council of Ministers deliberations (Section 8i)
Trade secrets and commercial confidential information (Section 8d)

fees.inspection_fee: Free for first hour, Rs 5 per hour after that
payment_modes: Online payment (Net Banking, UPI, Debit/Credit Card)
fees.section_reference: Section 6(1) and Section 7(5) of RTI Act 2005

time_limits.life_or_liberty_matter: 48 hours — for urgent matters involving life or liberty
time_limits.application_via_apio: 35 days — 5 extra days added when filed through APIO
time_limits.second_appeal_filing: Within 90 days of receiving first appeal decision

steps.title: Write the application
steps.description: Write a clear application in Hindi, English, or the official language of the area. Clearly mention what specific information you need. Be specific — do not write vague requests.
steps.tip: Ask for information from only one department per application

steps.title: Identify the PIO
steps.description: Find the name and address of the PIO (Public Information Officer) of the department from which you need information. Every government department must have a designated PIO.
steps.tip: PIO details are usually available on the department's official website

steps.title: Attach fees
steps.description: Attach Rs 10 as Court Fee Stamp or Indian Postal Order (IPO). BPL card holders must attach a copy of their BPL certificate instead of fees.
steps.tip: Cash payment is generally not accepted. Use IPO or Demand Draft.

steps.title: Submit the application
steps.description: Submit the application to the PIO in person or send via registered post. Always take an acknowledgment receipt.
steps.tip: Sending via registered post gives you a tracking number as proof of submission

steps.title: Wait for response
steps.description: You should receive a response within 30 days. For urgent matters involving life or liberty, the response must come within 48 hours.
steps.tip: Note the date of submission — 30 days are counted from that date

process_online.applicable_for: Central Government ministries and departments only. For UP State Government departments, use offline process or UP state portal.

steps.title: Visit portal
steps.description: Go to rtionline.gov.in and click on Submit Request

steps.title: Read guidelines
steps.description: Read the guidelines, tick the checkbox 'I have read and understood the guidelines' and click Submit

steps.title: Fill the form
steps.description: Select Ministry/Department. Fill your name, email, mobile number, and address. Write your information request in the text box (maximum 3000 characters).

steps.title: BPL status
steps.description: If you are BPL, select Yes and upload your BPL certificate PDF (max 1MB). Otherwise select No.

steps.title: Make payment
steps.description: Non-BPL applicants pay Rs 10 online via Net Banking, UPI, Debit/Credit Card, or RuPay Card.

steps.title: Save registration number
steps.description: After submission, you will receive a unique registration number. Save it for tracking your application status.

process_online.helpline_hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:30 PM

first_appeal.when: If no response received within 30 days, or if dissatisfied with the response
first_appeal.time_limit_to_file: Within 30 days of receiving response or expiry of 30-day period
first_appeal.to_whom: First Appellate Authority — a senior officer in the same department
first_appeal.online: File via rtionline.gov.in using Submit First Appeal option

second_appeal.when: If dissatisfied with first appeal decision
second_appeal.time_limit_to_file: Within 90 days of receiving first appeal decision

penalties.daily_penalty: Rs 250 per day if PIO refuses or delays without reason
penalties.maximum_penalty: Rs 25,000 maximum total penalty on PIO
penalties.disciplinary_action: Recommended if PIO repeatedly fails to provide information
penalties.who_imposes: Central Information Commission or State Information Commission

section_8_exemptions: Life or physical safety endangering information
Cabinet papers and Council of Ministers deliberations
exemptions.important_note: Even exempt organizations must provide information related to corruption and human rights violations

sample_templates.title: Basic RTI Application (Hindi)
sample_templates.use_case: General information request from any government department
sample_templates.template:
सेवा में,
जन सूचना अधिकारी
[विभाग का नाम]
[पता]

विषय: सूचना का अधिकार अधिनियम, 2005 की धारा 6(1) के अंतर्गत सूचना प्राप्त करने हेतु आवेदन

महाशय/महाशया,

मैं [आवेदक का नाम], निवासी [पूरा पता], इस आवेदन के माध्यम से निम्नलिखित सूचनाएं प्राप्त करना चाहता/चाहती हूं:

1. [सूचना का विवरण — स्पष्ट और विशिष्ट रूप से लिखें]
2. [यदि और सूचना चाहिए तो यहां लिखें]

आवेदन शुल्क: रुपये 10/- कोर्ट फीस स्टाम्प के रूप में संलग्न है।

भवदीय,
[नाम]
[हस्ताक्षर]
[दिनांक]
[मोबाइल नंबर]

sample_templates.title: RTI for Ration Not Received (Hindi)
sample_templates.use_case: When ration has not been received from fair price shop
sample_templates.template:
सेवा में,
जन सूचना अधिकारी
जिला पूर्ति अधिकारी कार्यालय
[जिला], उत्तर प्रदेश

विषय: राशन वितरण से संबंधित सूचना हेतु RTI आवेदन

महाशय,

मेरा राशन कार्ड नंबर [XXXX] है। पिछले [X] महीनों से मुझे उचित मूल्य दुकान से राशन नहीं मिल रहा है।

मुझे निम्नलिखित सूचनाएं चाहिए:
1. मेरे राशन कार्ड पर पिछले 6 महीनों में कितना राशन आवंटित हुआ?
2. क्या मेरे कार्ड पर राशन वितरित दिखाया गया है?
3. उचित मूल्य दुकानदार के विरुद्ध क्या कार्रवाई की जा सकती है?

संलग्न: Rs 10/- कोर्ट फीस स्टाम्प

भवदीय,
[नाम, पता, दिनांक, मोबाइल]

sample_templates.title: RTI for Pension Not Received (Hindi)
sample_templates.use_case: When pension payment is delayed or stopped
sample_templates.template:
सेवा में,
जन सूचना अधिकारी
[संबंधित पेंशन विभाग]
[पता]

विषय: पेंशन भुगतान से संबंधित सूचना हेतु RTI आवेदन

महाशय,

मेरा पेंशन खाता संख्या [XXXX] है। पिछले [X] महीनों से मेरी पेंशन नहीं आई है।

मुझे निम्नलिखित सूचनाएं चाहिए:
1. मेरी पेंशन रोकने का क्या कारण है?
2. पिछले 12 महीनों का पेंशन भुगतान विवरण
3. पेंशन कब तक जारी होगी?

संलग्न: Rs 10/- कोर्ट फीस स्टाम्प

भवदीय,
[नाम, पता, दिनांक, मोबाइल]

sample_templates.title: RTI for Scholarship Not Received (Hindi)
sample_templates.use_case: When scholarship amount has not been credited
sample_templates.template:
सेवा में,
जन सूचना अधिकारी
समाज कल्याण विभाग / छात्रवृत्ति विभाग
[जिला], उत्तर प्रदेश

विषय: छात्रवृत्ति भुगतान से संबंधित सूचना हेतु RTI आवेदन

महाशय,

मेरा छात्रवृत्ति आवेदन संख्या [XXXX] है, शैक्षणिक वर्ष [XXXX]।

मुझे निम्नलिखित सूचनाएं चाहिए:
1. मेरा छात्रवृत्ति आवेदन किस स्तर पर लंबित है?
2. छात्रवृत्ति कब तक जारी होगी?
3. यदि आवेदन अस्वीकृत है तो कारण बताएं

संलग्न: Rs 10/- कोर्ट फीस स्टाम्प

भवदीय,
[नाम, पता, दिनांक, मोबाइल]

faqs.q: What is RTI and when to use it?
faqs.a: RTI (Right to Information) Act 2005 gives every Indian citizen the right to ask for information from any government department. Use it when you face delays in ration, pension, scholarship, road construction, or any government scheme.

faqs.q: Can RTI application be filed in Hindi?
faqs.a: Yes. RTI application can be filed in Hindi, English, or the official language of the area. Section 6(1) of the RTI Act clearly allows this.

faqs.q: What is the fee for filing RTI?
faqs.a: Application fee is only Rs 10. BPL card holders are completely exempt from fees. First appeal is free. Document copies cost Rs 2 per page extra.

faqs.q: How many days to get RTI response?
faqs.a: Response must come within 30 days. If the matter involves life or liberty of a person, response must come within 48 hours.

faqs.q: What to do if no response received?
faqs.a: File a First Appeal within 30 days. This is completely free. If that also fails, file a Second Appeal with UP State Information Commission (phone: 0522-2237838) within 90 days.

faqs.q: Is it mandatory to give reason for filing RTI?
faqs.a: No. Section 6(2) of RTI Act clearly states that applicant is not required to give any reason for requesting information.
Yes, for Central Government departments via rtionline.gov.in. Payment can be done online via Net Banking, UPI, or Card. For UP State Government departments, offline process is used.

faqs.q: Where to file second appeal in UP?
faqs.a: UP State Information Commission in Lucknow. Phone: 0522-2237838. For Central Government matters, appeal goes to Central Information Commission (CIC) in New Delhi.

faqs.q: What if PIO refuses to give information?
faqs.a: File First Appeal first. If that fails, file Second Appeal with UP SIC or CIC. The Commission can impose a penalty of up to Rs 25,000 on the PIO.

faqs.q: What information cannot be obtained through RTI?
faqs.a: National security, cabinet decisions, personal privacy, court-forbidden information, intelligence agencies (IB, RAW), and trade secrets are exempt. But corruption and human rights violations can be asked even from intelligence agencies.

central.name: RTI Online Portal Helpline
central.hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:30 PM

central.name: Central Information Commission (CIC)
central.address: August Kranti Bhawan, Bhikaji Cama Place, New Delhi 110066

uttar_pradesh.name: UP State Information Commission (SIC)
uttar_pradesh.note: For second appeals and complaints against UP State Government departments

key_sections.section_3: All citizens shall have the right to information
key_sections.section_6: How to make a request for obtaining information
key_sections.section_7: Disposal of request — 30 day time limit rule
key_sections.section_8: Exemptions — what information cannot be provided
key_sections.section_19: Appeal process — first and second appeal
key_sections.section_20: Penalties on PIO for non-compliance — up to Rs 25,000

common_mistakes: Asking for information from wrong department — always identify correct PIO first
Writing vague requests — always be specific about what information you need
Forgetting to attach fees — application will be rejected without Rs 10
Not keeping receipt — always get acknowledgment of submission
Filing RTI for state departments on rtionline.gov.in — that portal is for central govt only
Missing appeal deadlines — first appeal must be filed within 30 days
